title: tephrosia purpurea subsp. apollinea
intro: Tephrosia purpurea subsp. apollinea, also known as the Apolline tephrosia, is a plant species in the pea family that is native to tropical regions of Africa. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Tephrosia purpurea subsp. apollinea include its potential to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its ability to improve digestion and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, Tephrosia purpurea subsp. apollinea can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
